attacking_style: Operates from long-range breakaways (80km+) and demonstrates resilience by recovering from temporary distancing. Finishes with climbing-sprinter combination to win from reduced groups.
team_tactics: Limited data on team setup, but showed independent capability to survive in breakaways and self-manage efforts without apparent domestique support in final.
terrain_preference: Suits stages where combination of climbing endurance and sprint finish are required. Thrives in reduced sprint scenarios from breakaways rather than bunch kicks.
vulnerability: Can be temporarily distanced on climbs within breakaways, suggesting threshold limitations when pace intensifies, though shows recovery capacity.
key_patterns: Targets long-range breakaway opportunities established early in stage (80km+ to go).
Demonstrates recovery ability after being dropped, regaining contact in final kilometers.
Excels in small-group sprint finishes combining climbing fatigue resistance with speed.
form_trajectory: positive: Successfully converted breakaway opportunity into Grand Tour stage win with strong finish
